name: Chris Potter
description: American jazz saxophonist, composer, and multi-instrumentalist
abstract: Chris Potter (born January 1, 1971) is an American jazz saxophonist, composer, and multi-instrumentalist. Potter first came to prominence as a sideman with trumpeter Red Rodney (1992–1993), before extended stints with drummer Paul Motian (1994–2009), bassist Dave Holland (1999–2007), trumpeter Dave Douglas (1998–2003) and session work, w…

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: Chris Potter, Don Byas, Frank Wess, and the list goes on, Ben Webster, Coleman Hawkins, Georgie Auld, they all play the tenor saxophone but they all have — Al Cohn, Zoot Sims — I could go on all day — Tina Brooks, Hank Mobley. You know they play one note and you know who they are immediately.

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I got him involved in some things in Europe — things using a string quartet and everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve played with Chris Potter before but we’ve never actually played as a group together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: But so is Chris Potter. Chris Potter knocks me out too. Now Chris is working, he’s doing very well, he’s getting records out...
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Sandke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: There’s Chris Potter, there’s Joe Lovano, there’s Billy Charlap. I mean they all know their deficiencies incidentally.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I saw that what’s-his-name just got it – the kid – Potter – Chris Potter just got that.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
